technical_documents.description: A high-yield circular farming blueprint that leverages a single low-wattage solar pump to elevate water once, triggering a continuous zero-electricity gravity cascade. By combining bio-filtration with floating-raft Deep Water Culture, this system slashes conventional operational energy demands by 80% while maximizing organic crop and protein yields per square meter.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Conventional aquaponics systems use continuous high-wattage pumps (150W+) for recirculation, making them economically unviable in off-grid communities where electricity costs exceed fish revenue.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: By executing a single-lift hydrodynamic loop, a ultra-efficient 25W DC pump powered by an integrated 50W solar panel raises nutrient-rich water to the apex header tank (2.5m head vertical lift). From this point, the fluid volume navigates a multi-tiered gravity cascade through four descending Deep Water Culture (DWC) floating raft beds. Interconnected mechanical bell siphons orchestrate automated flood-and-drain cycles, generating passive kinetic aeration that maximizes dissolved oxygen saturation (>5 mg/L) without requiring dedicated aeration grid infrastructure.

bill_of_materials.name: 4 x 200L IBC Totes (cut as grow beds)

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Cut IBC totes horizontally to create four grow beds and one fish tank at the lowest level.
Step 2: Stack beds at descending heights (60cm, 45cm, 30cm, 15cm) on a sturdy timber rack.
Step 3: Install bell siphons in each bed; plumb overflow from each tier to the tier below.
Step 4: Position solar pump in fish tank sump; run discharge pipe to the highest grow bed.
Step 5: Fill with dechlorinated water; cycle for 4 weeks with ammonia to establish nitrifying bacteria before adding fish.

comments.text: Running tilapia + lettuce for 8 months. 25W pump handles everything. Electric bill is zero.
